extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIC Primoz won the Stage 10 ITT by 25 seconds over Bevan, gaining 3:06 on Quintana to take red jersey
tactical_patterns: BEVAN Patrick emerged as unexpected GC threat, moving to second overall with ITT performance, revealing CCC GC ambitions.
CAVAGNA Remi's 45.7 km/h speed at 24 years signals emerging TT generation can sustain power on technical courses.
QUINTANA Nairo's 3:06 loss versus 2:00 target shows deteriorating TT performance under race fatigue, predictable GC vulnerability.

rider_signals.BEVAN Patrick: positive: Moved to second overall via ITT, showing genuine GC legs beyond expected breakaway domestique role
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: 46+ km/h average confirms pre-race favorite status, demonstrating no fatigue from opening nine stages
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: Lost red jersey with 3:06 deficit after targeting sub-2:00 loss, suggests declining form pattern in Grand Tours
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Remi: positive: 45.7 km/h speed at 24 years old establishes him as generational TT talent, fastest intermediate splits
rider_signals.LOPEZ Miguel Angel: neutral: Motivation spike when caught by Roglic suggests psychological vulnerability under pressure in ITTs
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: positive: Top-15 ITT finish in first Grand Tour reveals hidden GC potential beyond expected stage hunting role
